evidence_text: reaction temperature (900°C)
evidence_summary: The comparison changes catalyst precursor decomposition atmosphere, not CNT growth temperature. Only 900 °C growth was reported, so no growth-temperature window or temperature effect can be assigned.
notes: tags=temperature_window;growth_temperature; Prevents the three catalyst-preparation conditions from being misread as a CNT growth-temperature study.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_001

source_locator: PDF pp. 4-5 and 8; Table 1; Figs. 2-4; Conclusions
evidence_text: The size of the dark spots was measured and found to be in the range of 3-6 nm
evidence_summary: Ar and H2 decomposition produced better-dispersed 3-6 nm Fe-Mo clusters than air decomposition; the paper links this preparation difference with higher yield and more homogeneous t-MWCNT products.
notes: tags=thermal_decomposition_atmosphere;Fe-Mo_dispersion;particle_size; Provides a directly testable catalyst-preparation variable without adding a new main-table field.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_002

evidence_text: non-uniform distribution of catalyst particles
evidence_summary: Air-decomposed SG-1 showed phase separation, particle agglomeration, low BET area, 73% weight-gain yield, 43% TGA carbon, and a mixed t-MWCNT/MWCNT/carbon-fiber product.
notes: tags=air_decomposition;phase_separation;agglomeration;mixed_product; Preserves a negative comparison that can guide catalyst-preparation screening.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_003

evidence_text: Mo provides a shielding effect for the Fe nanoparticles
evidence_summary: The authors propose that high Mo content shields Fe nanoparticles against agglomeration and promotes methane aromatization; the paper does not provide a lower-Mo run series that isolates the effect.
notes: tags=Mo_shielding;Fe_agglomeration;methane_aromatization; Captures the proposed role of Mo while clearly retaining its hypothesis status.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_004

source_locator: PDF pp. 2 and 8; Introduction; Sections 2.2 and 3.3
evidence_text: suitable for scale-up to the kilogram level
evidence_summary: The source describes sol-gel preparation as suitable for kilogram-level scale-up and uses large-scale wording for a reactor of about 100 mm inner diameter, while each reported CNT run loads only 0.1 g catalyst.
notes: tags=sol-gel;kilogram_scale_claim;reactor_scale;throughput_gap; Retains the scale-up claim together with the experimental-load caveat for later industrial review.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_005

evidence_summary: The process uses CH4/H2 at 900 °C; SG-3 catalyst preparation additionally uses H2 at 700 °C. The paper gives the gas program but no process-safety assessment.
notes: tags=CH4;H2;high_temperature;safety_gap; Flags high-temperature flammable-gas handling for later scale-up review without assigning an unsupported risk score.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_006

evidence_text: Carbon % yield @TGA
evidence_summary: The reported 43%, 85% and 82% values are TGA carbon contents of as-synthesized products, not purified application-grade CNT purity or separately measured residual-metal content.
notes: tags=TGA_carbon;purity_definition;residual_metal; Prevents incompatible purity metrics from being compared as equivalent values.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_007

evidence_text: the higher Mo content for the Fe-Mo/MgO catalyst induced a higher product yield
evidence_summary: The authors associate the high-Mo Fe:Mo ratio of 1:1.5 with increased carbon yield and more uniform t-MWCNTs, but this paper does not present a controlled lower-Mo composition series.
notes: tags=high_Mo;yield;t-MWCNT_uniformity;composition_series_gap; Preserves a catalyst-composition hypothesis suitable for future controlled experiments.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_010

evidence_summary: TEM-EDS spot atomic ratios differ from the nominal bulk Fe:Mo ratio; they are localized particle measurements and were not promoted to bulk composition fields.
notes: tags=TEM-EDS;localized_composition;bulk_composition_caveat; Retains potentially useful particle-level chemistry without mislabeling it as bulk catalyst composition.; legacy_observation_id=OBS_DUBEY_2012_008

evidence_text: Mo has a strong interaction with Fe
evidence_summary: XPS binding-energy shifts and Mo oxidation-state assignments provide mechanistic context for atmosphere-dependent Fe-Mo interaction, but are not expanded into new v0.1 main-table fields.
